message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** A bot-filed documentation gap issue for `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` 1.0.0-beta.3, noting that the README is missing documentation for chat isolation enforcement, session ID response header, metadata validation constraints, and updated `previous_response_id`/`conversation.id` validation behavior introduced in beta.2 and beta.3.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (1.0.0-beta.3)
**Affected API:** README / Troubleshooting section
**Scenarios:**
- Users implementing multi-tenant servers are unaware of the `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header and its 404-on-mismatch behavior
- Clients not forwarding or logging the `x-agent-session-id` response header may miss session correlation information
- Users supplying metadata may receive unexpected 400 errors without knowledge of the 16-key/64-char/512-char limits
- Troubleshooting section does not document new 404/400 cases added in beta.2 and beta.3

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` to document the above behaviors and expand the Troubleshooting section with new error entries.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue; no code defect is reported. The issue body includes a detailed implementation guide with exact markdown to add.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review CHANGELOG entries for beta.2 and beta.3 of `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` to confirm all behavioral changes are captured
2. Apply the README edits described in the issue body (chat isolation, session ID header, metadata constraints, troubleshooting entries)
3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` and `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` to verify no unintended changes
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` is a client-side SDK library (not Azure.ResourceManager.* or Azure.Provisioning.*), and the issue concerns SDK documentation behavior
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — CODEOWNERS maps `/sdk/agentserver/` to `%AI Agents` (line 85); confirmed by quality reference issues #57183, #55585, #55561 all using "Client" + "AI Agents"
- **Confidence:** High — package, service directory, and CODEOWNERS entry are unambiguous; multiple quality reference issues corroborate the label pair
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — service label "AI Agents" matched this entry; scanning bottom-to-top, no more-specific multi-label entry for "AI Agents" exists
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ankitbko, @RaviPidaparthi
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; issue left unassigned (ServiceOwners-only path); "needs-team-attention" not added (issue is bot-filed, not "customer-reported")
- **Scan notes:** All management-specific entries (lines 912+) require "Mgmt" — not applicable. The `%AI Model Inference` (line 94) and `%AI Projects` (line 100) entries don't match "AI Agents". First match encountered bottom-to-top was line 85.
</details>
